title: pelargonium × majale
intro: Pelargonium × majale, also known as the Red Tip Geranium, is a hybrid plant species within the geranium family, combining the characteristics of Pelargonium cucullatum and Pelargonium lanceolatum. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The leaves and stems of Pelargonium × majale contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and soothing digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Pelargonium × majale can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
